Dear Goenkans

All the best for Math PT 1 Exam. Let's recapitulate the following points discussed during the revision

1. Rough Work Column to be made as soon as you get the answer sheets.
2. Read the questions carefully during the reading time before attempting them. Specially for MCQ's the options are always too close. Use the rough space to do calculations wherever required.
3. Copy the digits and signs correctly for the numbers
4. Show proper steps wherever needed
5. Write statements for word problems
6. Do not forget to write the units with the answers wherever needed.
7.Utilise you time judiciously. Keep time for revision to check the calculations.
8. highlight answers at the end and draw finishing lines if you have time.

All the best once again! Believe in yourself, you all will do great!

Dear Students and Parents,

This is a gentle reminder regarding birthday celebrations in school for Class VII.

Birthday celebrations are to be kept simple and meaningful. Expensive return gifts or gift items are strictly not permitted in school. Students may bring only a small stationery item, a sweet, or a chocolate to share with classmates. The value of the item should not exceed approximately ₹30.

Further, for students of Classes VI to VIII, kindly note that as per the school guidelines and almanac rules already shared during presentations and meetings, students must come to school in proper school uniform on all school days, including their birthdays. Casual clothes are not permitted for birthday celebrations in these classes.

We request all students and parents to kindly adhere to these guidelines so that the rules are followed fairly and uniformly by everyone.

Thank you for your cooperation and support.
Regards
Class teacher

Dear Parents,


This is a gentle reminder that students ( Class VI upwards)  are not permitted to come to school dressed in casual clothes on their birthdays. Students are required to attend school in the prescribed school uniform only, even on birthdays.


We request your cooperation in ensuring adherence to the school guidelines.


Regards,
Class Teacher

Subject: Harvard Undergraduate
Science Olympiad – India Edition (2026–27)

Dear Students and Parents,

Students of Grades 7–10 are invited to participate in the Harvard Undergraduate Science Olympiad – India Edition (2026–27) in
collaboration with Learn with Leaders.

Key Information:

  • Eligibility: Grades
    7–10

  • Fee: INR 2360 (inclusive
    of GST)

  • Registration Deadline: 30th
    May 2026

Competition Format:

Round 1 (Online Exam):

  • Duration: 3 hours (MCQ-based)

  • Subjects: Biology, Earth Science, Chemistry,
    Physics, Mathematics

  • Dates:

  • Grades 7–8: 14th November 2026

  • Grades 9–10: 15th November 2026

Round 2 (In-Person – New
Delhi, Jan 2027):

  • Top 100 students (50 per category) qualify

  • Includes 4-day STEM bootcamp with Harvard mentors

Recognition:

  • All participants: Certificate

  • Top 100: Certificate, medal, STEM bootcamp

  • Top 10: Trophy, distinction medal, scholarships
    (incl. USD 200 Learn with Leaders)

  • Winner: Fully funded Harvard University summer camp

Students interested should register before the
deadline. Refer to the attached brochure/flyer for details.

Warm Regards

G.D Goenka Public School Sec-48, Gurgaon

⭐ *Mindful May: Protecting Nature, Reducing Waste!* ⭐

As May begins, it’s time to turn awareness into action and care for the world around us. 🌿🌍
Our *May Toolkit* for *Classes 3 to 8* is designed to inspire students with hands-on, meaningful activities that build responsibility towards nature and sustainable living. ✨♻️

This month, we shine a light on important global days like:

🦋 🐢*International Day of Biodiversity*
🚫🛍️ *International Plastic Free Day*

Each activity encourages students to explore how their choices impact the environment, whether it’s protecting biodiversity or reducing plastic use in daily life. Small actions today can create a big difference for our planet tomorrow. 🌱💚

🔗 *May Toolkit Link:*

Classes 6 to 8 - https://gosharpener.com/posts/1260688

Let’s empower students to care for nature, reduce waste & become responsible changemakers, because a better future begins with mindful choices today. 🌍✨

*Warm Regards,*
*Team GoSharpener

📢 Reminder for Students – Don’t Miss This Opportunity!

Dear Students,
You are encouraged to participate in the Summer Book Writing Festival (SBWF) 2026 – the world’s largest free online summer camp for young authors (Grades 3–12), organised by BriBooks and EducationWorld.
✨ Why you should join:
- Write and publish your own original book
- Receive a Participation Certificate
- Attend free masterclasses on book writing
- Get a chance to win national recognition and awards
🏆 Top achievers can get:
Featured on Discovery Channel & NDTV 24x7
Book launch at the World Book Fair, New Delhi
Recognition at international book festivals (Dubai, Sharjah, London, New York, Singapore)
Features in digital media, podcasts, and BriBooks platforms
💡 Best part? It’s completely FREE!
Make the most of your summer—explore your creativity and become a young author!
Warm regards

Registration link
https://www.camp.bribooks.com/events/student/signup/sc-india-2026?sid=139013

A GENTLE REMINDER



🎉✨ LET THE HOUSE SPIRIT BEGIN!✨🎉

Dear Students,

As we step into an exciting new academic session 2026–27, it’s time to gear up for one of the most thrilling parts — INTER-HOUSE COMPETITIONS!

Get ready to showcase your talent, explore your interests, and shine like never before! Whether you’re an artist, a performer, a sportsperson, or a thinker — there’s something for everyone!

Here’s what you need to do:
You will be choosing TWO competitions for the session:
➡️ ONE from Section A
➡️ ONE from Section B

SECTION A – Choose ANY ONE

Art 🎨 | Music 🎶 | English 📖 | Science 🌿 |
Sports 🏏 (Cricket, Tennis, Swimming, Yoga) |
Math 🔢 | French 🇫🇷

SECTION B – Choose ANY ONE

Dance 💃 | Theatre 🎬 |
Sports ⚽ (Football, Table Tennis, Badminton, Basketball) |
Hindi 📚 | ICT 💻 | Sanskrit 🪔, SSC
Talent wins games, but teamwork and participation win hearts!

So think wisely, choose what excites you, and get ready to represent your house with passion and pride!

Fill in the Google Form and submit your choices today as the last date was April 12, 2026.

Google form link : https://forms.gle/tkUT69FsT8QnsY9d6

Let the competition begin… may the best house win!

Dear Students

PRACTICE QUESTION 2
Write a descriptive paragraph (100-120 words) on Virat Kohli. Include details about his early life, cricket career, achievements, and personality. Use descriptive words and phrases to make your paragraph interesting and engaging. Use adjectives
• Born on 5 November 1988 in Delhi, India Showed interest in cricket from a young age
Known for aggressive batting style and determination
Became captain of the Indian cricket team
• Achieved numerous records in international cricket
Awarded several cricketing honors, including ICC awards Disciplined, hardworking, and passionate about the game
• Inspires young cricketers and fans worldwide
• Leads by example on and off the field
